methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Xaira Therapeutics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Seed), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Amber Bio is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Basem Al-Shayeb and Jacob Borrajo, operating within a broader Biotech & Health sector universe of 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Xaira Therapeutics, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics.

Only one valuation method proved applicable given data constraints. The stage-progression approach uses Amber Bio's $26M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $65M. Peer-median methodology could not be executed due to insufficient narrow-filter comparables in the Biotech & Health sector—while 14 same-sector peers exist, fewer than 3 met same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier criteria simultaneously. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (6.1x median across the three public tickers) remains inapplicable without the target's disclosed revenue.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $55M to $75M, centered at $65M. This range reflects the single applicable method and its inherent uncertainty: the 2.5x multiplier is standard but not precise to Amber Bio's specific risk profile, capital efficiency, or technology maturity. Key uncertainties include the absence of comparable private financings at identical stage and the lack of revenue data, which would anchor public multiples. A more refined valuation would benefit from recent seed-stage financing rounds or disclosed revenue figures.
